creator: Alexander Terp
description:
The loop context variable specified with `with <name>` persists after the loop ends, retaining its final context object value. If a variable with the same name existed before the loop, it is permanently overwritten.

**Repro:**
```
ctx = "important"
for i in range(3) with ctx:
    pass
print(ctx)  // {"idx": 2, "src": [0, 1, 2]} (NOT "important")
```

**Root cause:** `core/interpreter.go` line 1029: setLoopContext uses i.env.SetVar() which unconditionally sets the variable in the current scope. There is no save/restore of the previous value and no cleanup after the loop.

**Test files:** `bugs/control_flow/112_context_var_leak.rad`
title: Loop context variable (with) leaks after loop and overwrites existing vars
